evals.name: critical-cross-module-dependency
evals.expected_output: Should identify critical errors: direct injection of EmailSender from Notifications module, synchronous cross-module call that should be event-driven. Should suggest using MediatR to publish a CampaignCreatedEvent instead.

assertions.text: Identifies direct import of EmailSender from another module as an error
Identifies synchronous cross-module service call as critical violation
Recommends using MediatR events instead of direct calls
Includes corrected code example showing event-driven pattern
Explains why this pattern breaks modularity and prevents future microservices extraction

evals.name: suboptimal-di-registration
evals.expected_output: Should identify warning: services should be registered in their respective modules' RegisterServices() method, not in ContainerBuilderExtensions. Should suggest each module implements IModule and registers its own services.

assertions.text: Identifies that services are registered in ContainerBuilderExtensions instead of in module classes
Classifies as WARNING (works, but violates pattern) rather than ERROR
Recommends moving services to each module's RegisterServices() method
Shows example of IModule implementation with RegisterServices()
Explains why module-level registration is important (loose coupling, independent modules)

evals.name: correct-event-driven-pattern
evals.expected_output: Should show PASS. This correctly demonstrates: (1) event-driven communication via MediatR, (2) proper module boundaries with no cross-module imports, (3) publishing integration events that other modules can subscribe to, (4) clean separation of concerns.

assertions.text: Reports overall status as PASS with no errors or warnings
Confirms correct use of MediatR for event publishing
Verifies no direct cross-module service dependencies
Identifies and highlights positive aspects (event-driven communication, proper module boundaries)
Explains why this pattern enables future microservices extraction and independent testing
